behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: Integration (embedder module + index schema check). PASS2-AUDIT: Rejected Integration because a stub embedder would always return expected length and miss real model/output-shape drift; behavior depends on real AI/ML output from the embedding model. FINAL: Agentic. Rejection-gate: System/Workflow rejected because behavior text is only a single embedding output shape check, not multi-agentic sequencing and not primarily OS/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real embedding model with fixed queries, then assert returned vector length exactly matches live index dimension configured in the vector DB. Fail on any mismatch.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: Integration (search service + vector store wiring + score ordering contract). PASS2-AUDIT (Integration-brake): (a) real OS/network/subprocess required? No, can use in-process/local vector index fixture. (b) depends on real AI/ML output quality? No, can use fixed vectors. (c) multiple agentic steps? No. (d) LLM-stage handoff? No. FINAL: Integration. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior text is ordering by score, which is reproducible with local module wiring; Agentic/Workflow rejected because no LLM/model judgment is required for assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a local test index with deterministic vectors, run similarity search through the service layer, and assert returned items are monotonic by cosine score (descending) and stable for ties per defined policy.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: Agentic. PASS2-AUDIT: Kept Agentic because correctness is about LLM judgment on nuanced intent; deterministic stubs would hide failures in actual re-ranking behavior. FINAL: Agentic. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because behavior text specifies one LLM re-ranking step, not multiple agentic decisions chained.
behaviors.test_description: Use a curated nuanced-intent query set with fixed candidate lists; invoke real re-ranker and assert at least one position changes from raw similarity order on designated cases, with run-to-run tolerance thresholds.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: Agentic. PASS2-AUDIT: Rejected Integration because the behavior is semantic relevance quality ('no close matches') tied to real embedding/model behavior; fixed mocks can force empty/non-empty and miss true semantic false positives. FINAL: Agentic. Rejection-gate: Workflow rejected because this behavior can be validated at the single model-dependent retrieval decision boundary, without requiring multi-agentic sequencing assertions.
behaviors.test_description: Run real embedding + retrieval on an adversarial 'no-match' query suite against a known corpus, assert top similarity stays below threshold and API returns empty results.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: System. PASS2-AUDIT: Upgraded to Workflow because latency covers a sequence with multiple real agentic steps (embedding model call + LLM re-rank) plus retrieval and API path; stubbing either model masks true median latency and pipeline interaction effects. FINAL: Workflow. Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected because behavior text is explicitly end-to-end across more than one model-dependent stage, not a single agentic decision.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production-like end-to-end load (real API, real embedding service, real vector DB, real LLM re-ranker) over representative median traffic; measure p50 latency and assert <2.0s over a stable sample window.
